title: Thirparappu Waterfalls
tagline: Thirparappu Waterfalls — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Thirparappu Waterfalls is located near Kulasekharam town in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu state, India.
overview.description: Thirparappu Waterfalls is located near Kulasekharam town in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu state, India. The Kodayar River makes its descent at Thirparappu. The waterfalls is about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) from Pechiparai Dam. It is just a 15 min drive from the waterfalls.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thirparappu
topPlaces.description: Thirparappu is a town located in the Kanyakumari District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a popular tourist destination.

topPlaces.name: Arumanai
topPlaces.description: Arumanai or Arumana is a Panchayat town in Kanyakumari district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. In olden days it is known as Arumana.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvarambu
topPlaces.description: Thiruvarambu is a village in the town of Thiruvattar town panchyat, Tamil Nadu. The Thirparappu Falls is 3 km from Thiruvarambu.

topPlaces.name: Kulasekaram
topPlaces.description: Kulasekharam is a town located in the district of Kanyakumari, Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the most important business centres in the district, after Marthandam.

topPlaces.name: Kadayal
topPlaces.description: Kadayal is a town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kodayar River
topPlaces.description: Kodayar is a river in the Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state, India, which originates from the Kodayar Lake. The vaavubhali festival is held on the river.

topPlaces.name: Vaikunda Chella Pathi
topPlaces.description: Vaikunda Chella Pathi is one among the oldest Nizhal Thangals in the Western Kanyakumari district. It was located 6 km North-east of Marthandam midway between Marthandam and Kulasekaram at Thiruvattar.

topPlaces.name: Puthenchanthai
topPlaces.description: Puthenchanthai is in Villavancode taluk, Kanyakumari District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a small village shared by Maruthancode & Edaikodu Panchayats.

Vaikunda Chella Pathi is one among the oldest Nizhal Thangals in the Western Kanyakumari district. It was located 6 km North-east of Marthandam midway between Marthandam and Kulasekaram at Thiruvattar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thirparappu Waterfalls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thirparappu Waterfalls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thirparappu Waterfalls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thirparappu Waterfalls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thirparappu Waterfalls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thirparappu Waterfalls?
faq.a: Stays in Thirparappu Waterfalls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thirparappu Waterfalls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thirparappu, Arumanai, Thiruvarambu, Kulasekaram, Kadayal.
